question_text: An astronaut on the Moon weighs 180 N, while on Earth she weighs 1080 N. The gravitational field strength on the Moon is 1.6 N/kg. What is the gravitational field strength on Earth?
explanation: Weight = Mass × Gravitational field strength. On the Moon: 180 = Mass × 1.6, so mass = 112.5 kg. On Earth: 1080 = 112.5 × g, so g = 9.6 N/kg (approximately 10 N/kg). This shows that gravitational field strength varies depending on location.
